text.title: stream-json-orchestration Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns that defeat the mid-run-reaction value of stream-json orchestration.

antipattern.symptom: Orchestrator calls `subprocess.run(..., capture_output=True)` or awaits `proc.stdout.read()` and then `json.loads(stdout)` — fails with `JSONDecodeError` because stream-json is line-delimited.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated stream-json as a single JSON document instead of a JSONL stream.
antipattern.fix: Use `subprocess.Popen(..., stdout=PIPE, bufsize=1, text=True)` and iterate `for line in proc.stdout`. Parse each line individually.

antipattern.symptom: Orchestrator detects tool calls by `re.search(r"using tool: (\w+)", line)` against pretty-print output.
antipattern.root-cause: Code predates the stream-json output mode; never migrated.
antipattern.fix: Switch the CLI to `--output-format stream-json` and read structured `tool_use` events. Regex against plain text breaks on every model version bump.

antipattern.symptom: Orchestrator queues events into a list/queue unboundedly; memory grows over long runs; downstream consumer falls behind without warning.
antipattern.root-cause: No backpressure between the parser and the downstream sink (UI, telemetry, replay log).
antipattern.fix: Use a bounded `asyncio.Queue(maxsize=256)` or block on `put()` when the consumer lags. Surface backpressure as a metric.

antipattern.symptom: `claude -p "task" --output-format stream-json` invoked without `--allowedTools` or `--max-turns`. Agent loops forever or uses unexpected tools.
antipattern.root-cause: Author tested locally with one task and didn't add defence-in-depth before shipping.
antipattern.fix: Make `--allowedTools` and `--max-turns` mandatory constructor arguments in the orchestrator class. CI test rejects an invocation that omits either.

antipattern.symptom: Orchestrator returns when stdout closes; never matches against `event.type == "result"`. Loses the final cost, duration, and `result_subtype`.
antipattern.root-cause: Code uses `for line in proc.stdout` and exits when the iterator stops, ignoring the structured terminator.
antipattern.fix: Pattern-match `if event.get("type") == "result"` inside the loop and set the run summary from that event. Treat absence of a result event as `error_during_execution`.
